title: Preparing an Emergency Kit for Winter Power Outages
summary: A basic winter emergency kit takes little time to assemble but matters enormously if a power outage happens during cold weather.
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Preparing an Emergency Kit for Winter Power Outages | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: A basic winter emergency kit takes little time to assemble but matters enormously if a power outage happens during cold weather.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Backup power for essential medical equipment is the top priority, if applicable.
Warm blankets, flashlights, and a battery-powered radio round out the basics.
A plan for refrigerated medication is worth having in advance.

paragraphs: For anyone relying on powered medical equipment (oxygen concentrators, certain mobility aids), a battery backup or a clear plan for where to go during an extended outage is the single highest-priority item in a winter emergency kit.

Beyond that, warm blankets, flashlights with extra batteries, a battery-powered or hand-crank radio, and a few days of non-perishable food and water round out a reasonable basic kit — along with a plan for keeping refrigerated medications cool if the outage is extended.

nextSteps: Prioritize backup power planning for any medical equipment first.
Assemble basic supplies: blankets, flashlights, batteries, radio.
Have a specific plan for refrigerated medications during an extended outage.
disclaimer: General preparedness guidance — anyone using critical medical equipment should discuss a specific backup plan with their equipment provider.
